Off Calendar Jul-11-2024 Off Calendar

DEFENDANT BRADFORD CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (CONT, FROM 6/28/24)
Off Calendar Jul-01-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David P. Wasserman, Esq., 415-567-9600 dave@wassermanoffices.com for attorney for Defendant Bradford Construction Inc.
No Other appearances.

No hearing is held. Ot Contested/ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRADFORD CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (CONT, FROM 6/28/24)

Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend. Judgment shall be entered in favor of defendant forthwith.

On May 29, 2024, the court denied plaintiffs' motion for summary judgment as a matter of law. The court concluded that plaintiffs were not entitled to disgorgement under California Business and Professions Code section ("B&P") 7031(b) because defendant's license was reissued and defendant met the doctrine of substantial compliance under B&P 7031(e). Per Evidence Code section 452(d), the court takes judicial notice of this order. This prior adjudication disposes of plaintiffs' sole claim. Plaintiffs do not contend otherwise but assert that defendant's common law motion for judgment on the pleadings is untimely and improper. The law, however, is unclear whether Code of Civil Procedure section 438 displaces the nonstatutory motion. In Tung v. Chicago Title Co. (2021) 63 Cal.App.5th 734, 757, the court expressly refused to decide the issue and numerous courts have recognized the common law motion after the Legislature promulgated CCP 438 (effective January 1, 1994). (See Stoops v. Abbassi (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 644, 650; Bezirdjian v. O'Reilly (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 316, 321; Singhania v. Uttarwar (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 416, 435.) There is no basis to send this case to trial in light of the court's prior ruling. Therefore, the court entertains this common law motion and concludes that defendant is entitled to judgment.

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
